name: Fun Facts
description: Weird trivia, world records, and the kind of facts you whip out at a party.

questions.q: Which marsupial has fingerprints nearly indistinguishable from human ones?

source.notes: So similar that police forensics have actually been concerned about contamination at crime scenes in Australia.

questions.q: What is the national animal of Scotland?

source.notes: On the Royal Coat of Arms since the 12th century.

questions.q: Which animal famously plays dead when threatened?
Which US President got famously stuck in a White House bathtub?

source.notes: Likely apocryphal but legendary. He weighed ~340 lbs.

questions.q: What is the smallest country in the world?
Which English word has no vowels (excluding Y)?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced distractors Crystal/Cyst/Ply (which also lack vowels except Y) with words containing true vowels. All-consonant words usually require Y as a semi-vowel — 'rhythms' uses Y.

questions.q: What was the original color of carrots before selective breeding?

source.notes: Modern orange carrots were bred in the Netherlands in the 17th century.

questions.q: Which condiment was once sold in the 1830s as a digestive medicine?

source.notes: Tomatoes were considered medicinal; sold as 'tomato pills.'

questions.q: What is the most-stolen food item globally?

source.notes: About 4% of global cheese production is stolen, per a 2011 study.

questions.q: What's the term for an irrational fear of vegetables?
About how much does an average cumulus cloud weigh?

source.notes: Density × volume; clouds are made of small water droplets, but there are a lot of them.

questions.q: What was Coca-Cola's original color when first produced in 1886?

source.notes: Common myth says green; it was always brown from the caramel coloring.

questions.q: Which Parisian landmark was originally intended to be temporary?

source.notes: Built for the 1889 World's Fair, originally meant to be dismantled in 1909.

questions.q: Which US President delivered the Gettysburg Address?
Which barrier built in 1961 became a Cold War symbol of German division?
Which culinary umami comes from fermented soybeans, key to miso soup?

source.notes: Soy sauce is also fermented soybeans, but miso paste is the soup's main ingredient.

questions.q: What ancient civilization is credited with inventing ice cream?

source.notes: Frozen dairy treats date back to the Tang Dynasty (~618-907 CE).

questions.q: What's the British nickname for police officers, derived from Sir Robert Peel?

source.notes: audit-corrected 2026-05-09: replaced 'Peelers' (also a valid Peel-derived nickname) with 'Tommies' (British soldiers). 'Bobbies' is the modern nickname; from Robert Peel's first name.

questions.q: Which iconic NYC nickname dates from a 1920s sportswriter's John J. Fitz Gerald column?

source.notes: Fitz Gerald popularized the term in a horse racing column.

questions.q: A group of crows is collectively known by what ominous-sounding name?
Which planet in our solar system spins on its side, with an axial tilt of about 98 degrees?
Honey found in ancient Egyptian tombs was discovered to still be what?
Which is the only mammal capable of true sustained flight?
A bolt of lightning is roughly how many times hotter than the surface of the Sun?
What is the only letter that does not appear in any U.S. state name?
Bananas are slightly radioactive because they contain a natural isotope of which element?
What is a baby kangaroo called?
Octopuses have how many hearts?
The Great Wall of China is famously NOT actually possible to do which of these?
choices: See it with the naked eye from space

questions.q: A 'baker's dozen' refers to which number?
Which country has the most natural lakes in the world?
The fear of long words is humorously given what notoriously long name?
What everyday object's name comes from the Czech word 'robota,' meaning forced labor?
A 'jiffy' is sometimes defined in physics as an actual unit of time roughly equal to what?
Which fruit was historically so expensive in 17th-century Europe that people rented them to display at parties?
Wombats are unique in the animal kingdom for producing droppings of what unusual shape?
The dot over a lowercase letter 'i' or 'j' has what specific typographical name?
What is the name for the plastic or metal tip at the end of a shoelace?
The shortest war in recorded history, the Anglo-Zanzibar War of 1896, lasted approximately how long?
What is the technical term for the 'brain freeze' you get from eating something cold too quickly?
The space between your eyebrows is anatomically called the what?
Scotland's national flower, often worn as an emblem, is the what?
A snail can sleep continuously for how long under the right conditions?
What is the collective noun for a group of flamingos?
The Eiffel Tower can grow taller in summer by roughly how much due to thermal expansion of its iron?
